text: Starlink connecting schools in Bolivia https://t.co/WZGBIaJM4j
ai_summary: Elon Musk announced that Starlink is expanding its satellite internet connectivity to schools in Bolivia, demonstrating real-world deployment of the service beyond developed markets. This highlights Starlink's business strategy of penetrating underserved markets and expanding addressable market through infrastructure projects in developing regions, while showcasing a practical application of satellite internet technology for education access and digital inclusion.

Starlink connecting schools in Bolivia https://t.co/WZGBIaJM4j

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) June 6, 2026

text: Two Falcon flights in 19 hours https://t.co/hkTg8ostVa
ai_summary: Elon Musk announced that SpaceX successfully conducted two Falcon rocket flights within a 19-hour window, demonstrating rapid launch cadence and operational efficiency. This milestone is significant for SpaceX's business model as it showcases the company's ability to execute frequent launches, which is critical for revenue generation, satellite deployment (Starlink), and establishing SpaceX as a reliable launch provider in the commercial space industry.
embed_html:
Two Falcon flights in 19 hours https://t.co/hkTg8ostVa

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) June 6, 2026

text: @jawwwn_ @60Minutes There is obviously no “degree” you can get from a university that actually teaches you how to make an orbital rocket, as none of the professors know how to do it!
summary: @jawwwn_ @60Minutes There is obviously no “degree” you can get from a university that actually teaches you how to make an orbital rocket, as none of the professors know how to do it!

parent.text: @jawwwn_ @60Minutes I didn’t “think I was qualified”. There was no other choice. No one good would join a rocket startup as chief engineer that they thought was destined for failure.
parent.name: Elon Musk
parent.embed_html:
I didn’t “think I was qualified”. There was no other choice. No one good would join a rocket startup as chief engineer that they thought was destined for failure.

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) June 6, 2026

ai_summary: Elon Musk explains SpaceX's early hiring challenges and unconventional path to building rocket technology, noting that no qualified aerospace engineers would join a startup deemed destined to fail, and that traditional university degrees don't actually teach orbital rocket design since professors lack hands-on experience. The insight reveals how SpaceX overcame talent acquisition barriers through necessity-driven innovation and self-directed learning, relevant to early-stage deep tech startups facing similar credibility and recruitment challenges.
embed_html:
There is obviously no “degree” you can get from a university that actually teaches you how to make an orbital rocket, as none of the professors know how to do it!

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) June 6, 2026

summary: @jawwwn_ @60Minutes I didn’t “think I was qualified”. There was no other choice. No one good would join a rocket startup as chief engineer that they thought was destined for failure.

parent.text: Many people forget that @elonmusk is a self-taught aerospace engineer. Elon explains why he thought he was qualified to be the CTO of SpaceX: “I read a lot of books, and talked to a lot of smart people.” Via @60Minutes
parent.name: Jawwwn
parent.embed_html:
Many people forget that @elonmusk is a self-taught aerospace engineer.

Elon explains why he thought he was qualified to be the CTO of SpaceX:

“I read a lot of books, and talked to a lot of smart people.”

Via @60Minutes https://t.co/DuMrhmSpPY pic.twitter.com/SbL5ZwnYgW

— Jawwwn (@jawwwn_) June 6, 2026

ai_summary: Elon explains his decision to become SpaceX's chief engineer despite lacking formal aerospace credentials, arguing that no qualified engineer would join a startup perceived as destined to fail. The insight reveals a critical hiring and leadership lesson for early-stage startups: founders often must fill key technical roles themselves when the venture's success is uncertain, and conviction about the mission can substitute for traditional credentials when external talent won't take the risk.
embed_html:
I didn’t “think I was qualified”. There was no other choice. No one good would join a rocket startup as chief engineer that they thought was destined for failure.

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) June 6, 2026
